tweets.text:
LinkedIn is not a professional network. It's a language.

I built a translator. Here's what it does. 🧵

veda.ng/lit

Nobody you know speaks like this in real life.
Yet somehow, on LinkedIn, it's the only language.

So I asked: what if we just... admitted that?

My personal favourite.

You took a nap. You are not optimising anything. You were tired.
LinkedIn disagrees.

The product launch one broke me.

"Had an intimate, high-signal gathering." Zero people showed up. The translator felt nothing.

I got arrested for fraud.
"Thrilled to announce I'm starting a new chapter."

At some point the platform became a parody of itself. I just made it official.

Try it: veda.ng/lit

text:
States are trying to force identity checks into your phone's Operating System. GrapheneOS is saying no.

This isn't just about privacy. It's about who owns the hardware. If a state tells your phone when it can or can't run code, it isn't your device anymore.

The kernel is the last wall. Once it's gone, your device belongs to the government.

@vedangvatsa

Companies are done hiring for 'CS degrees.' They want builders who build products fast.

The hard part isn't writing code anymore; the tools handle that. The hard part is knowing what to build and why it matters.

Ideas are the new code. Builders are the new engineers.

@vedangvatsa

The move toward asteroid mining marks the end of zero-sum thinking.

Scarcity has always been a policy choice. By moving resource extraction to space, we decouple growth from planetary damage.

The next century belongs to those who look up, not down.

@vedangvatsa

Google is purging the low-quality junk made by machines.

If you spent a decade building real expertise, you just won. The web is finally getting harder for bots to game.

When information is free, proof of effort is the only thing with value.

@vedangvatsa

We can now pull 1,000 liters of water from dry air every day.

We don't need pipes or plants anymore. Just the air around us. This is what happens when we solve for the physical world, not just digital pixels.

The next big wealth will come from the atmosphere.

@vedangvatsa

Web 2 was about attention. Web 3 is about delegation.

You won't go online to browse. You'll go online to tell an agent to get something done. Bookings, research, deals—handled.

The human provides the goal. The machine provides the result.

@vedangvatsa

The kernel is the new border.

If we let states bridge that gap for 'safety,' we lose the personal computer forever. A device that can't say no to its creator is not yours.

Encryption is a human right. So is hardware ownership.

@vedangvatsa

AI agents can't open bank accounts, but they can hold crypto wallets.

Stablecoins are the native payment rail for the machine era. We're moving to a world where software does more deals than humans.

The financial web is being rewired for agents.

@vedangvatsa

When context is scarce, authenticity becomes a search signal.

We are drowning in content but starving for truth. The platforms that survive will be the ones that verify, not just aggregate.

Trust is the only asset that doesn't scale with compute.

@vedangvatsa
